frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions emerge from temporal integration of stimulus pulses to a bound

frameworks.name: joint neural-behavioral generative model
frameworks.one_line: unified framework fitting choices and multi-neuron spikes simultaneously per region

frameworks.name: behavior-only accumulation model
frameworks.one_line: accumulator fit to choices alone, contrasted with neural-derived accumulators

core_question: Do different rat brain regions implement distinct accumulation dynamics, and how do these relate to the animal's choice?
key_assumptions: choices arise from accumulation of pulse-based evidence to a decision
per-region spiking activity reflects a region-specific accumulation process
behavioral and neural data can be jointly modeled within one framework
manipulations: recording region (PPC vs FOF vs ADS)
data source used to fit the accumulator (neural vs behavioral)
key_findings: each region was best fit by a distinct accumulation model
all region-derived models differed from the choice-derived model
FOF activity favored early evidence; ADS reflected near-perfect accumulation
ADS showed more instances of decision vacillation than FOF
interpretations: different regions differentially reflect choice-related accumulation signals
an accumulation-based framework predicts choices better when combining all regions
